item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 141 replies — 121.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 141 • Updated: 2026-06-23 17:10 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

62395063
>>62373752
>I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.
Yes, going to college or vocational school and getting a higher paying job is probably the correct path for the vast majority of you.
>t. 32yo engineer making $135k
Go for STEM and if you can't cut it then pick a trade that interests you. If I didn't make it in STEM I was considering being an Air Traffic Controller.

62395577
Brainstorming

Software created current-state AI, AI created current state demand for hardware resources, hardware surge created demand for production materials, hardware costs contribute to higher cost datacentre build costs, running the datacentres with current state AI running on current state hardware consumes higher energy demand.

>AI - priced in
>Hardware - priced in
>Materials - priced in
>Datacentres - priced in
>Energy - priced in

So the disruptor to this ecosystem might be sitting where we started - software development. The race to highly energy-efficient AI models is the next logical thing. The current trajectory is unsustainable from a resources perspective, and the financial burn rate is unsustainable.

You make AI consumption highly resource efficient, all of those things come down as the demand reduces. Or does supply just increase to fill the increased capacity?

That takes me to the next innovative era that will consume the increased capacity;
> Robotics and humanoids / androids - building interest but probably not fully priced in yet
> Transport and automated movement - AI powered, driverless vehicles (short Uber)
> Highly efficient reusable rocketry - transport anything anywhere quickly (reusability powered by AI robotics)
> Personal digital self's - Consumer facing AI-version-of-you running your life, in your house, ordering your groceries - your life on autopilot (think a personal JARVIS)
> medical application - localised AI models in hospitals and medical rooms - control robotics in surgery
> mobile devices - uncertain future, at some point the personal handheld device model may come to an end

So my take away for a macro, strategic tech play could be to accumulate software and tech players with resources that can produce highly efficient AI models that use less resources - Microsoft is trading at a discount? - instead of over-committing to hardware and the fundamentals. Model efficiency could improve at any stage.

Thoughts?

Small business owners would also love this. Imagine a traveler who stays at an Airbnb month long for business and uploads his AI to manage the boring stuff of his new "home" while he acclimates to the business and travel of the area.

https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1132599

>Publishing in the Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA) online June 22, the study team found that online portal messages more than doubled between 2020 and 2025 (by 153 percent). By contrast, total telephone calls decreased by 6 percent over the same period.

>“Clinical staff will need to be trained in mastering the tools of messaging in healthcare; in using AI support programs, including chatbots that can frame content to minimize its complexity; and in making the most effective use of clinician time needed for online billing and online counseling,” said Dr. Segev, who is also a profession in NYU Grossman’s Department of Population Health.

>Already, he noted, NYU Langone uses AI support tools to speed up drafting of physician and provider notes.

>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

62398220
>>62397819
>Chainlink
>Decentralized
Decentralized does not have to mean the cypherpunk fantasy of "banks aren't needed and everyone is a sovereign individual." It's largely a matter of semantics.
Decentralized simply means that the nodes participating in the network are independent of one another.
If I tell you to watch a football game and report the result back to me in exchange for money, it's difficult to guarantee that you're telling the truth.
If I tell you and another person—whom you don't know—to watch the same game and report the result back to me, my chances of learning the true result increase, because the two of you would have to find each other, coordinate, and risk not getting paid.
Now imagine I tell 20 people to do the same, and on top of that, each person must deposit $1,000 to be eligible to participate and earn money. Nobody has an incentive to lie, because doing so would put their deposit, any future payments for their service, and their reputation as football-game reporters—all of which generate income for them—at risk. That's a decentralized oracle network.
This is the meaning of decentralization and what Chainlink is building - consensus without a chain. Consensus as a service.
I'm done spoon feeding for today. Hope that was clear and helpful to all the perplexed and confused anons here.

I was in a similar position like you OP. I disagreed with the staff engineer, he bad mouthed me to manager and I landed on a PIP. I spent 6 months on a PIP, looking for a job (I'm in Germany, they can't really fire me).
Mental was the hardest part. This is a process designed to break you down. What really helped me was to disengage with the work process, say nothing, do the bare minimum, wear my poker face.
After 6 months, I found a new job, negotiated an exit with a severance package.
I am not gonna lie, this was a shitty experience, but I learned a lot from it.

I wish you best of luck OP. Don't let them wear you down.

>>62388676

This. Nothing good comes easy to normal people. You have to put in the work to earn it.

You should pick some goals, even 1 to start then break it down into achievable, smaller goals.

Eg you want to be beautiful (however that isn't measurable so first make it a measurable goal) then get some feedback from doctors eg in Korea to determine what procedures you would need and how much they cost and how long that will take you to save up for, plus what order you can do them in. And what can you do in the meantime eg around tongue posture.

Do the same for your body and diet.

Start trying things that make you uncomfortable eg scuba diving or rock climbing, things that push you a bit.

You're so deep into a "woe is me" cycle that you think everything is pointless but the truth is you haven't tried enough things to have any idea what is even possible and enjoyable for you. You have enough money to start loving your life, so get to it.
